name: Dick Katz
abstract: Richard Aaron Katz (March 13, 1924 – November 10, 2009) was an American jazz pianist, arranger and record producer. He freelanced throughout much of his career, and worked in a number of ensembles.

interviews.title: Dick Katz interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Carter
about_quotes.evidence: with Jimmy Garrison on base and Dick Katz on piano, and Joe Jones on drums and John Collins on guitar, just with the 8 piece group, we made that 'Further Definitions.'

about_quotes.speaker: Candido Camero
about_quotes.evidence: The pianist was . . . Dick Katz on piano... That was my first recording.
about_quotes.doc_title: Candido Camero — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Jones
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (Sam Jones) does not speak about Dick Katz; the interviewer mentions Dick Katz as a piano player in a context involving Miles Davis and the Jazz Prophets, but Sam Jones provides no personal interaction or relationship details regarding Katz.

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: I was involved with something called the American Jazz Orchestra... this was a band comprised of mostly big band veterans... Dick Katz... It was a great band. And we started doing concerts down at Cooper Union
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Derek Smith
about_quotes.evidence: he got me and I was representing England, and he got a French pianist by the name of René Urtreger, and we all played like four tracks. And Dick Katz was there who was the American piano player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Derek Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 29, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Schaap
about_quotes.evidence: one night I went down there and I knocked on his door and he blindfold tested me... So I said, 'The piano player, that’s Dick Katz.'... And he said, 'Oh yeah, that’s my father.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Schaap interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003
